system.job: You are analyzing test results from assessments of Large Language Models to choose a best-of-breed selection.
activity: You are providing an aggregate analysis of the model test results.
The goal is to select LLMs for use in a CPU-based environment (desktop, tablet, cell), typically 8 to 16 GB of RAM.
The analysis should identify best-of-breed in terms of overall performance, accuracy, formatting, inferencing, resource consumption, and optimization.
Limit the response to the JSON output model.

all_models: list all models under consideration from best to least
output.justification: explain why best-of-breed model was selected

alternative_selections.details: explain how model was identified as an alternate

RESULTS.justification: The granite4:1b model was selected as the best-of-breed for a CPU-based environment with 8-16 GB of RAM because it offers the optimal balance of speed, efficiency, and accuracy. Unlike the larger 3b and 4b models, which score lower on performance and resource consumption (3/5), the 1b model maintains a high accuracy score of 4/5 while achieving superior performance (4/5) and resource efficiency (4/5). This makes it ideal for mobile and desktop CPU inference where memory bandwidth and processing power are limited, ensuring fast response times without significant degradation in output quality.

alternative_selections.details: Identified as a strong alternative for scenarios where higher reasoning depth is required and resource constraints are less strict. It maintains high accuracy (4/5) and formatting (5/5) but sacrifices speed (3/5) and resource efficiency (3/5) compared to the 1b variant.

Selected as an alternative for its solid overall performance (4/5) and stability across configurations. It shares similar resource profiles to other 3b models, making it a viable backup if specific architectural preferences favor Mistral-based models.
